description: Seed candidate inventory for comparative mapping of game-theory, simulation, agent-based modelling, system-dynamics, and adjacent modelling ontologies against UOGTO.

sources.name: Kinetic Simulation Algorithm Ontology
sources.inclusion_rationale: KiSAO is directly relevant to simulation algorithm semantics and SED-ML algorithm references.

sources.name: Simulation Experiment Description Markup Language
sources.inclusion_rationale: SED-ML describes models, simulations, tasks, data generators, outputs, and KiSAO-backed algorithms that overlap UOGTO execution semantics.

sources.name: Minimum Information About a Simulation Experiment
sources.discovery_route: seed: COMBINE simulation experiment reporting standard
sources.inclusion_rationale: MIASE provides reporting requirements for simulation experiments that can be compared with UOGTO model/session/trace coverage.

sources.name: Ontology for Simulation, Modelling, and Optimization
sources.discovery_route: seed: modelling and simulation ontology search
sources.inclusion_rationale: OSMO is explicitly about simulation, modelling, and optimization and is likely to overlap UOGTO model/execution/binding semantics.

sources.name: European Materials and Modelling Ontology
sources.inclusion_rationale: EMMO is a modelling-oriented upper/reference ontology used by materials modelling and simulation ontology ecosystems.

sources.name: Virtual Materials Marketplace Ontologies
sources.inclusion_rationale: VIMMP ontologies model services, models, interactions, and marketplace semantics around computational modelling.

sources.name: Game Ontology Project
sources.inclusion_rationale: The Game Ontology Project is a structured game concept vocabulary relevant to UOGTO's game specification and outcome concepts.

sources.name: Game Description Language and GDL resources
sources.inclusion_rationale: Game Description Language models game rules, states, legal moves, and terminal conditions that overlap UOGTO rules/actions/outcomes.

sources.name: XMILE System Dynamics Standard
sources.inclusion_rationale: XMILE captures stock-flow/system-dynamics model structure and is relevant to UOGTO dynamic and simulation modules.

sources.name: DEVS formalism and DEVS metamodel resources
sources.inclusion_rationale: DEVS concepts such as atomic/coupled models, events, state transitions, and simulation time overlap UOGTO dynamics and execution traces.

sources.name: High Level Architecture Federation Object Model resources
sources.inclusion_rationale: HLA/FOM resources are relevant to distributed simulation object, interaction, federation, and execution interoperability semantics.

sources.name: ODD protocol for agent-based models
sources.inclusion_rationale: ODD captures overview/design concepts/details of agent-based models and can be mapped to UOGTO specifications, agents, state, and processes.

sources.name: KAoS ontology and policy services
sources.inclusion_rationale: KAoS is relevant to multi-agent permissions, obligations, policy, and agent coordination semantics adjacent to UOGTO deontic and agent modules.

sources.name: PROV-O
sources.inclusion_rationale: PROV-O overlaps UOGTO execution provenance, traces, activities, agents, generated outcomes, and source attribution.

sources.name: P-Plan ontology
sources.inclusion_rationale: P-Plan models plans, steps, variables, and executions, overlapping UOGTO executable-game and simulation workflow semantics.

sources.name: OWL-Time
sources.inclusion_rationale: OWL-Time supports temporal intervals and instants relevant to sessions, traces, event timing, and simulation time.

sources.name: SSN/SOSA
sources.inclusion_rationale: SSN/SOSA observation, procedure, result, and system semantics can align with simulation observation/output traces.

sources.name: schema.org
sources.inclusion_rationale: schema.org overlaps UOGTO metadata, creative work, software, dataset, action, and web-discoverability semantics.

sources.name: Basic Formal Ontology
sources.discovery_route: seed: upper ontology used by many applied ontologies
sources.inclusion_rationale: BFO is relevant as an upper ontology for interpreting overlap with process, object, role, and temporal semantics.

sources.name: DOLCE
sources.inclusion_rationale: DOLCE provides foundational categories useful when comparing events, qualities, agents, and social objects across modelling ontologies.

sources.name: OntoUML / Unified Foundational Ontology
sources.discovery_route: seed: upper ontology and conceptual modelling
sources.inclusion_rationale: OntoUML/UFO can inform conceptual modelling comparisons for agents, events, relations, roles, and social commitments.
